symbols: Solomon enthroned — the wise king receiving divine wisdom to discern truth from deception
sword raised over the infant — the test that revealed the true mother through her sacrificial love
two mothers — the contest between genuine love and possessive deception
throne and court — the justice of God mediated through righteous human authority
description: A sepia ink wash drawing depicting King Solomon seated on an elevated throne in a grand architectural setting with draped curtains and columns. He wears royal robes and gestures with authority. Before him, a dramatic scene unfolds: a soldier raises a sword over an infant on the ground while two women react — one reaching desperately toward the child, the other standing back. Numerous courtiers and attendants crowd both sides of the composition. The drawing style is loose and gestural with warm brown washes creating depth and atmosphere.
theological_description: The Judgement of Solomon is one of the most famous episodes in the Old Testament, recorded in 1 Kings 3:16-28. Two women who lived in the same house each bore a son, but one child died in the night. Both claimed the living child was theirs. Solomon commanded that a sword be brought and the living child be cut in two, with half given to each woman. The true mother immediately cried out to give the child to the other woman rather than see him killed, while the false claimant agreed to the division. Solomon thus identified the true mother by her sacrificial love. This episode demonstrated the extraordinary wisdom God had granted Solomon in answer to his prayer in 1 Kings 3:9: 'Give your servant a discerning heart to govern your people and to distinguish between right and wrong.' The narrative became the paradigmatic example of godly wisdom applied to human affairs. Solomon's wisdom was a divine gift (1 Kings 3:12), foreshadowing the greater wisdom of Christ, who Paul calls 'the wisdom of God' (1 Corinthians 1:24). The true mother's willingness to lose her child rather than see him destroyed also prefigures the self-sacrificial love at the heart of the Gospel.

scripture_refs.reason: The Judgement of Solomon — two mothers, the sword, and the wise verdict — the exact event depicted
Solomon's prayer for a discerning heart, the divine gift that enabled this judgement
God granting Solomon a wise and discerning heart unlike any before or after him
Christ as the wisdom of God — Solomon's wisdom foreshadowing the greater wisdom incarnate in Christ
The Lord gives wisdom — Solomon's judgement as the demonstration of divine wisdom in human governance
